claim: Taiwan TSMC endgame most likely: political destabilization/coup without full invasion, allowing China to creep influence while avoiding war consequences
actionable: Monitor Taiwan political party shifts (DPP vs KMT) as geopolitical proxy for TSMC supply chain risk; DPP pro-US/independence, KMT pro-China-friendly.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Even with KMT (China-friendly) government in Taiwan, US export controls are enforced via American banking systems and equipment ecosystems, so TSMC must comply regardless
actionable: Understand that geopolitical pressure on Taiwan's government does not automatically bypass US export controls due to structural dependency on US financial systems.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Roughly one-third of AI lab researchers are Chinese; antagonizing Chinese talent with overly strict export controls risks brain drain from US AI labs
actionable: Balance security concerns with retaining top talent; overly aggressive China-specific policies may trigger researcher departures to competitors or home countries.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Chinese AI models like Deepseek and Qwen are 'quite impressive' and competitive; open-sourcing alone doesn't make a model inferior, it's a strategic choice
actionable: Don't dismiss open or restricted models based on openness alone; evaluate on capability, alignment with use case, and cost trade-offs.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Hyperscaler capex ($180B Google, $200B Amazon for 2025) is 4x previous years because they see insane demand, not speculative bubble; market reaction (stock down) reflects misunderstanding
actionable: When evaluating hyperscaler investments, track capex announcements against inference demand signals (Anthropic revenue, GitHub Copilot adoption) rather than stock price alone.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Claude Code adoption went from 2% to 4% of GitHub commits in one month (January 2026); total AI-assisted code ~10%+ across all tools
actionable: Track AI code generation adoption metrics as a leading indicator of enterprise AI readiness and productivity gains.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Hyperscalers face innovator's dilemma: AI (including agents, code assistants) threatens their core revenue (search, ads, office productivity) while forcing massive capex bets
actionable: For large tech companies, design incentive structures to ensure AI cannibalization is rewarded internally; failure to do so can delay competitive response.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Hyperscalers will likely have zero profit/positive free cash flow in 2027; they'll spend every dollar on AI infrastructure and model training, not shareholder returns
actionable: Expect mature hyperscaler profitability to decline 2026-2027 as capex surges; only re-invest if you believe long-term AI returns exceed traditional business returns.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Meta already taking $40B debt to fund Louisiana AI cluster; hyperscalers will increasingly lever up for AI infrastructure since payoff horizon is years away
actionable: Monitor hyperscaler debt ratios and capex financing as proxy for AI intensity and confidence in long-term returns.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: General public and financial markets hate AI (opposed to job loss, inequality, environmental costs); backlash will likely become election issue, creating political risk
actionable: Plan for regulatory and political backlash against AI; anti-AI political platform may resonate with voters experiencing job displacement and inequality.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Jensen Huang embodies 'paranoid founder' (Andy Grove's 'only the paranoid survive') by rapidly pivoting strategy when sensing competitive threats
actionable: As a leader, actively scan for emerging threats and be willing to pivot strategy quickly; paranoia about disruption drives faster innovation cycles than incremental planning.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Nvidia pivoting to heterogeneous chip portfolio (GPUs, CPX for prefill/context, Gro for specialized tasks) rather than one-size-fits-all approach
actionable: Plan inference infrastructure around heterogeneous chips for different workload phases (prefill, decode, specialized); specialization improves latency and cost.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Nvidia faces business model deficit vs. vertical integrators (Google, Amazon, Meta); must innovate faster and better than competitors by more than just a little to justify margins
actionable: If competing against vertically integrated players, ensure your marginal advantage exceeds their integration cost savings; incremental improvements won't survive the gap.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Semiconductor fab capacity is the ultimate bottleneck for AI scaling through 2026-2028; building fabs (3-5 year lead times) slower than hyperscaler demand growth
actionable: Plan AI capex around semiconductor bottleneck, not power or data center buildout; fab capacity will constrain growth longer than other infrastructure.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Power bottleneck being solved via creative on-site generation (Elon's industrial gas engines, reciprocating diesel engines) instead of waiting for grid upgrades
actionable: For high-compute data centers, explore on-site power generation (microturbines, gas generators, even mobile units) as faster alternative to grid expansion.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Current semiconductors constraint shifted from production capacity (2023-2024) to power (2025) to fab capacity again (2026+); bottlenecks are sequential and shift yearly
actionable: Plan infrastructure investments year-by-year, anticipating the next bottleneck; avoid betting all resources on solving the current constraint.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking

claim: Companies hiring in AI infrastructure (SemiAnalysis model) see opportunities in tracking AI adoption metrics (usage, cost, who's using what), not just model benchmarks
actionable: Build internal dashboards tracking AI adoption metrics (commits, model usage, cost-per-query) rather than relying on published benchmarks for decision-making.
source_title: Dylan Patel Explains the AI War While Cooking | In-Context Cooking
